role_summary: Joint oral history of John and Jean Rosenberg, a civil rights legal/movement partnership; in this corpus's embedding their testimony sits at the MAXIMUM Southern Struggle reading (+1.00) in the entire 136-interview corpus, alongside top semantic neighbors Robert L. Carter (NAACP LDF) and Charles McLaurin (Mississippi SNCC).
ai_reading: The Rosenbergs' position on the Southern Struggle vs Northern Struggle axis is +1.00, the absolute MAXIMUM toward Southern Struggle in the entire 136-interview corpus, the second-largest extremum the catalog has documented (after Elmer Dixon's -1.00 on the nonviolence axis and Michael McCarty's -1.00 on the Southern axis itself). Their top semantic neighbors are Robert L. Carter (entry 108, count 230), Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 219), Richard Barry Sobol (entry 104, count 189, a civil rights attorney in Louisiana), Rosie Head (entry 111, Mississippi rural), and Simeon Booker and Moses Newson (entry 9, the journalist joint interview). The cluster reads as the deeply-Southern legal-civil-rights cohort, with the Sobol pairing particularly notable: Sobol was lead counsel on Sobol v. Perez (1968), the federal case that established Louisiana lawyers' right to represent civil rights defendants over local-bar objections. The embedding may be catching shared Louisiana-and-Mississippi legal-organizing vocabulary across the Rosenbergs and Sobol; their post-1965 work in Appalachia would be the additional regional dimension not captured by the catalog's five-axis projection.
biographical_paragraph: This is a joint oral history at the Library of Congress as entry 61 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Joint interviews in the corpus combine the testimony of two or more interviewees in a single recording; the catalog page treats the embedding signature of the joint transcript as one unit (see the AI's reading above). Independent biographical sourcing for each individual interviewee is pending; future enrichment may split this entry into separate per-person pages if individual biographical material becomes accessible.

sources.title: John and Jean Rosenberg (joint interview) oral history
